title: Physics for Mortals
subtitle: We unravel the mysteries of the universe
author: Wordsus Team
description: Discover the secrets of the universe in this complete guide to 365 physics lessons. From Newton's laws to quantum mechanics and the multiverse, explained in a simple way for everyone.

chapters.title: The Quantum Cat: Alive and dead at the same time
chapters.description: Explore Schrödinger's famous thought experiment and discover how bizarre quantum superposition works in physics at the microscopic level.

chapters.title: Spooky Action: The mystery of entanglement
chapters.description: Dive into bizarre quantum entanglement, where two particles connect instantly across distances, defying our established laws of the universe.

chapters.title: Double Slit: Between the world of waves and particles
chapters.description: Discover the strange wave-particle duality through the double-slit experiment and how mere observation alters the behavior of matter itself.

chapters.title: Quantum Tunneling: Passing through great invisible walls
chapters.description: Learn about quantum tunneling, a phenomenon where subatomic particles manage to pass through impenetrable physical barriers like simple ghosts.

chapters.title: Uncertainty: The universe and its absolute limits
chapters.description: We analyze Heisenberg's uncertainty principle to understand why it is completely impossible to measure our universe with absolute precision.

chapters.title: Teleportation: Quantum travel without moving at all
chapters.description: Learn about quantum teleportation, a real method that transfers exact information between distant particles without physically moving matter.

chapters.title: Quantum Computing: The great future of processing power
chapters.description: Delve into quantum computing and discover how qubits solve problems in seconds that would take traditional computer systems absolute millennia.

chapters.title: Vacuum Fluctuation: Nothingness originating the cosmos
chapters.description: Absolute vacuum does not exist. We explore vacuum fluctuations and how pairs of particles and antiparticles constantly appear and disappear.

chapters.title: Zeno Effect: Freezing time just by looking closely
chapters.description: Decipher the amazing quantum Zeno effect, which demonstrates how a quantum system can completely halt its decay simply by being closely observed.

chapters.title: Quantum Foam: The turbulent fabric of the universe
chapters.description: We travel to the tiny Planck scale to understand quantum foam, where space and time transform into a highly turbulent and chaotic endless tide.

chapters.title: Time Dilation: Why time is not completely fixed
chapters.description: We explore the time dilation of relativity, where moving faster causes clocks to tick much slower from the perspective of a stationary observer.

chapters.title: Twin Paradox: Aging at completely different rates
chapters.description: We analyze the famous twin paradox, showing how traveling at speeds close to that of light generates huge biological differences in human age.

chapters.title: Spatial Contraction: The universe shrinking itself
chapters.description: Discover the fascinating length contraction, where fast-moving objects physically compress from the point of view of an external observer.

chapters.title: Simultaneity: The present moment is just a myth
chapters.description: We understand the illusion of temporal simultaneity: how two events that occur at the exact same time for one person happen at different times for another.

chapters.title: Einstein's Formula: The hidden energy inside mass
chapters.description: We break down the famous mass-energy equivalence equation, revealing that a tiny fraction of matter contains a massively gigantic hidden power.

chapters.title: Spacetime Fabric: Immense gravity as a trampoline
chapters.description: Delve into Einstein's concept of the spacetime fabric, where gravity is merely a geometric curvature generated by extremely massive objects.

chapters.title: Cosmic Limit: The unbreakable barrier of light
chapters.description: Discover why the speed of light in a vacuum is the absolute cosmic limit and why attempting to surpass it would require infinite energy.

chapters.title: Time Curves: Theoretical journeys into the past
chapters.description: Explore the theory of closed timelike curves, a mathematical solution of general relativity that would allow time travel by creating closed loops.

chapters.title: Tachyonic Particles: Racing against the speed of light
chapters.description: Meet tachyons, those hypothetical particles that travel exceeding the speed of light and that, in theory, would move backward through time.

chapters.title: Hidden Dimensions: The mysterious extra strange worlds
chapters.description: We talk about the additional spatial dimensions predicted by various physics theories, which would be microscopically rolled up all around us.

chapters.title: The Higgs Boson: The mysterious particle of mass
chapters.description: Discover the mystery behind the Higgs boson and its associated field, fundamental elements responsible for granting mass to all other particles.

chapters.title: Antimatter: The explosive twin of normal matter
chapters.description: Learn the secrets of antimatter, the opposite equivalent of normal matter that generates an annihilation of pure energy if both ever touch.

chapters.title: Ghost Neutrinos: Particles that just pass right by
chapters.description: We analyze elusive neutrinos, almost massless particles that constantly pass through your body and the entire planet without interacting with anything.

chapters.title: Confinement: The prisoners of the subatomic world
chapters.description: We explore quark confinement and why it is totally impossible to separate them in isolation within a proton without generating particles from nowhere.

chapters.title: Standard Model: The universal recipe of ingredients
chapters.description: Decipher with us the standard model, the most successful theory in physics that perfectly describes all particles and three fundamental forces.

chapters.title: Muon Anomaly: A huge mystery for modern science
chapters.description: We investigate the anomalous magnetic moment of the muon, a recent experimental result that directly challenges our current rules of physics.

chapters.title: String Theory: Vibration shapes the entire reality
chapters.description: Delve into the complex string theory, the mathematical attempt to unify all physics by suggesting that particles are small vibrating cosmic threads.

chapters.title: Quantum Gravity: The great holy grail of physics
chapters.description: We tackle the greatest existing theoretical challenge: quantum gravity, the attempt to make relativity and quantum mechanics work perfectly together.

chapters.title: Arrow of Time: The impossibility of going back
chapters.description: Discover why the arrow of time exists and how the laws of thermodynamics prevent us from remembering the future, giving the cosmos one direction.

chapters.title: Laws of Entropy: The inevitable chaos of the universe
chapters.description: Learn about entropy and the universe's fundamental tendency to move from order to disorder, guaranteeing that broken objects never fix themselves.

chapters.title: Maxwell's Demon: Mocking the laws of thermodynamics
chapters.description: We explore the brilliant thought experiment known as Maxwell's demon, an imaginary being that could defy the strict rules of thermodynamics.

chapters.title: Absolute Zero: Extreme cold halts the entire cosmos
chapters.description: We travel to absolute zero temperature to understand what happens when the thermal motion of atoms almost completely stops inside a material.

chapters.title: Negative Temperatures: Hotter than absolute infinity
chapters.description: Discover the strange realm of negative absolute temperatures in quantum systems, which technically turn out to be hotter than any positive one.

chapters.title: Chaos Theory: The hurricane unleashed by a butterfly
chapters.description: We analyze the butterfly effect within chaos theory, where the slightest initial change in a system colossally alters the whole future result.

chapters.title: Laplace's Demon: Is the future already fully written?
chapters.description: We debate determinism and Laplace's demon: if a mind calculated every atom, would it manage to predict the entire cosmic future with absolute accuracy?

chapters.title: Perpetual Motion: The great lie of free clean energy
chapters.description: Discover why basic physics makes it impossible to create perpetual motion machines that work without external sources, always conserving energy.

chapters.title: Superconductors: Electrical current lasting forever
chapters.description: We talk about superconductivity, an amazing state where cooled materials lose all their electrical resistance, flowing without losing any power.

chapters.title: Quantum Levitation: Floating with the Meissner effect
chapters.description: We experience quantum magnetic levitation, where superconductors expel fields to float motionlessly over powerful magnets without any friction.

chapters.title: Total Superfluidity: Liquids climbing up steep walls
chapters.description: Delve into the mysteries of superfluidity, where extremely cold fluids lose all their viscosity and manage to flow freely, defying standard physics.

chapters.title: Bose Condensate: The superatom of the laboratories
chapters.description: We explore Bose-Einstein condensates, that fifth state of matter where thousands of atoms fuse, behaving entirely like a single giant entity.

chapters.title: Time Crystals: Their structure in constant change
chapters.description: Be amazed by time crystals, a new and exotic state of matter that repeats its complex structural patterns perpetually forever through time itself.

chapters.title: Light Solid Smoke: The most ultralight aerogels made
chapters.description: Discover the incredible applications of artificial aerogels, known as solid smoke for being among the absolute lightest thermal insulators in the world.

chapters.title: Metamaterials: Creating true invisibility cloaks
chapters.description: We analyze the science behind metamaterials, complex microscopic designs that manage to bend electromagnetic light, opening the path to invisibility.

chapters.title: Superionic Ice: Extremely hot water and high pressure
chapters.description: We investigate superionic ice, a strange extreme state of water capable of flowing like liquid metal while remaining surprisingly hot at all times.

chapters.title: Casimir Effect: When the outer vacuum pushes you
chapters.description: Learn about the mysterious Casimir effect, where metal plates placed extremely close together in a vacuum attract each other, driven by virtual particles.

chapters.title: Sonoluminescence: Bright light created by sound waves
chapters.description: We observe incredible sonoluminescence, a rare phenomenon where acoustic vibrations collapse bubbles to generate intensely bright flashes of light.

chapters.title: Acoustic Levitation: Holding objects using just sound
chapters.description: Discover how acoustic levitation uses powerful sound waves to suspend small objects in the air, simulating a total and real absence of gravity.

chapters.title: Cymatic Magic: The hidden geometry behind music
chapters.description: We study the amazing patterns of cymatics and learn how powerful acoustic waves manage to structure sand, creating spectacular geometric designs.

chapters.title: Physical Holography: Capturing real space on flat planes
chapters.description: Understand how physical holography really works, using laser interference to store real three-dimensional information on incredibly flat surfaces.

chapters.title: Coriolis Effect: Why massive hurricanes spin so hard
chapters.description: We clarify the great myths of the Coriolis effect and how the Earth's inertial rotation affects the direction in which storms spin on a planetary level.

chapters.title: Triboelectricity: Great secrets of daily static shock
chapters.description: We look into the well-known triboelectric effect of static electricity and its deep subatomic mysteries that remain unexplained by simple friction.

chapters.title: Slow Pitch Drops: A huge ongoing mechanical experiment
chapters.description: We analyze the curious mechanical pitch drop experiment, demonstrating empirically that certain seemingly solid materials actually flow very slowly.
